seconds.
3.  Dump everything (ice and all) into a tiki mug or hurricane glass.
4.  Top with more crushed ice if needed.
5.  Garnish with a pineapple wedge, mint sprig, and a cherry. A dusting of nutmeg on top is traditional.
If you want it lighter and more refreshing, skip the cream of coconut and use 1 oz coconut water instead, then serve over pebble ice.

Serve & garnish: Pour into a chilled hurricane or Collins glass. Garnish with a pineapple slice and a maraschino cherry.

name: Lemon Drop Martini
instructions:
1.  Sugar the rim: Rub a lemon wedge around the rim of a chilled martini glass, then dip the rim in 1 tablespoons sugar (for rim) to coat. Set aside.
2. Shake: Combine 2 fluid ounces citrus vodka (or plain vodka), 0.8 fluid ounces fresh lemon juice, 0.5 fluid ounces triple sec or Cointreau, and 0.5 fluid ounces simple syrup in a cocktail shaker filled with ice. Shake vigorously for about 15 seconds
3. Strain & garnish: Strain into the prepared glass. Garnish with 1 pieces lemon wheel or twist (for garnish).

'Notes'

The type of orange liquor will change the sweetness of this drink. Triple sec is the sweetest option. I prefer a nice drier CuraÃ§ao. It doesn't have to be super expensive.

name: Gin Martini
instructions:
chill glass
shake ingredients in shaker with ice.
strain into chilled glass.

name: Pain Killer
instructions:
Add dark rum, pineapple juice, orange juice, cream of coconut, and ice to a cocktail shaker. Shake until the cocktail mixture is chilled and frothy.
Fill a cocktail glass with crushed ice. Strain the cocktail mixture into glass. Sprinkle with fresh ground nutmeg, If desired, garnish with an orange slice, pineapple slice and pineapple fronds.

name: Suffering Bastard
instructions:
Add the bourbon, gin, lime juice and bitters into a shaker with ice and shake until well-chilled (about 30 seconds).

Strain into a Collins glass over fresh ice.

Top with the ginger beer.

Garnish with a mint sprig.

name: Old Fashioned
instructions:
in a glass muddle sweetener/sugar and bitters.
Add ice
pour whiskey over ice and stir

name: Jungle Bird
instructions:
Add the rum, Campari, pineapple juice, lime juice, and demerara syrup into a shaker with ice and shake until well-chilled.

Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice.

Garnish with a pineapple wedge.

name: Zombie
instructions: Add all ingredients to mixing tin. Flash blend or shake with crushed/pebble ice. Dirty dump into favorite Zombie glass, or double old fashioned or Collins glass. Top with ice, if needed. Garnish with mint sprig.

name: Classic Martini
instructions: Stir or shake ingredients. pour into a chilled glass

name: Tom Collins
instructions: mix in glass top with soda water

name: Sezerac
instructions: Muddle cube and bitter in mixing glass. Add rye with ice. Stir until chilled. Pour into chilled glass.

name: Gimlet
instructions:
**Garnish: Lime or Cucumber**

1. Chill your glass: Put a coupe or chilled rocks glass in the freezer while you prep.
2. Muddle the cucumber: Add 4 English cucumber slices (¼ inch thick) to the bottom of your shaker. Muddle firmly but not aggressively — you want the juice and fresh flavor, not pulp. About 8–10 presses.
3. Build & shake: Add 2 fluid ounces London Dry gin (Tanqueray, Beefeater, or Hendrick's), 0.8 fluid ounces fresh lime juice, and 0.5 fluid ounces simple syrup (1:1) to the shaker. Fill with ice and shake hard for 12–13 seconds 
00:13 — cucumber needs a vigorous shake to fully integrate.
4. Double-strain & serve: Fine-strain into your chilled glass to catch all cucumber solids. Serve up in a coupe, or over a large rock if you prefer it long. Garnish with a thin cucumber wheel on the rim.

name: Oaxacan Old Fashioned
instructions:
1. Chill your glass: Place a rocks glass in the freezer, or fill it with ice water to chill while you build the drink.
2. Build in the glass: Add 1 teaspoons agave nectar (diluted 1:1 with water), 2 dashes Angostura bitters, and 1 dash Bittermens Xocolatl Mole bitters directly to a mixing glass. Add 1.5 fluid ounces reposado tequila (Espolón or Olmeca Altos) and 0.5 fluid ounces mezcal (Del Maguey Vida or Banhez). Fill with large ice cubes.
3. Stir: Stir slowly and deliberately for about 30 seconds 
00:30 — you want dilution and chill, not aeration. This drink should be silky, not watery.
4. Strain & express: Dump the ice water from your rocks glass and add one large ice cube. Strain the cocktail over it. Take 1 large orange peel, for garnish, hold it skin-side down over the glass, and flame it with a match or lighter — squeeze to express the oils through the flame, then rub the peel around the rim and drop it in.

name: Bay Breeze
desc: Tropical, fruity, and effortlessly refreshing — the drink that disappears fastest at any party.
instructions:
1
Ice your glass: Fill a highball glass or rocks glass with ice.
2
Shake: Add 1.5 fluid ounces vodka, 3 fluid ounces pineapple juice (fresh or good quality), 1 fluid ounces cranberry juice, and 0.5 fluid ounces fresh lime juice to a shaker with ice. Shake for about 10 seconds 
00:10
 — just enough to chill and combine.
3
Strain & garnish: Strain into your iced glass. Garnish with 1 lime wheel, for garnish on the rim.

name: Hemingway Daiquiri
instructions:
1
Chill your glass: Chill a coupe or Nick & Nora glass in the freezer while you prep.
2
Juice your citrus: Juice both 0.8 fluid ounces fresh lime juice and 0.5 fluid ounces fresh grapefruit juice fresh. This drink lives or dies on fresh citrus — don’t cut corners here.
3
Shake: Add 2 fluid ounces white rum (Bacardi Superior or Plantation 3 Star), 0.8 fluid ounces fresh lime juice, 0.5 fluid ounces fresh grapefruit juice, and 0.5 fluid ounces maraschino liqueur (Luxardo) to a shaker with plenty of ice. Shake hard for 12–13 seconds 
00:13
 — grapefruit benefits from a vigorous shake.
4
Strain & garnish: Double-strain into your chilled coupe. Express 1 grapefruit peel or lime wheel, for garnish over the top and either rest it on the rim or drop it in.
Notes
